operation: First, a rectangular base box with dimensions 20 units (X-axis length), 10 units (Y-axis width), and 2 units (Z-axis height) is created on the XY workplane. Next, a new workplane is set up on the top (positive Z direction) face of the base box. A closed 2D sketch is constructed on this workplane: start at the coordinate (-6, 0), draw an 8-unit radius arc to the point (6, 0), then draw a straight line segment to (6, -3), followed by another 8-unit radius arc to (-6, -3), then close the sketch profile. Finally, this closed sketch is used to perform a cut-through-all operation that removes material through the entire thickness of the base box.
geometry: The model is a thin, flat rectangular plate with a symmetric, open-sided curved C-shaped through slot cut out from its central area. The slot has evenly rounded upper and lower curved edges, with its open end facing one of the shorter edges of the rectangular plate, spanning most of the plate's width while leaving a consistent solid border around the remaining perimeter of the plate.
function: This is a low-profile slotted retainer plate designed to clip onto or hold cylindrical components of matching diameter, suitable for use as a light-duty guide, spacer, or quick-mount retainer for rounded shafts, wires, or similar parts.
